data.title: Sensata downgraded to Equal Weight after recent share price gains: Barclays
data.description:
Sensata Technologies (ST) was downgraded to Equal Weight from Overweight by Barclays due in part to its recent rally.

The share price of the Massachusetts-based producer of sensors for various industries has surged nearly 50% year to date.

"Much of the appreciation in the share price, we believe, has been due to enthusiasm regarding emerging data center exposure at ST, but this is not likely to contribute meaningfully to revenue until mid-2027," said Barclays analysts in a Friday note. "Near-term muted auto production (~60% of sales) is a headwind despite LSD% outperformance. We also note that a long-standing activist shareholder exited ST in Q1."

Barclays set a $53 price target on Sensata.

Earlier this month, Truist Securities upgraded Sensata to Buy from Hold and raised its price target to $58 from $43 due to its growing exposure to AI and data center infrastructure.

data.title: Global rate cuts still lead hikes, but momentum is fading - BofA
data.description:
Central banks around the world continue to ease monetary policy this year, though the pace of rate cuts is losing steam as inflation concerns linger.

According to BofA Global Research, there have been 31 rate cuts versus just 12 hikes year to date, indicating that easing still leads the global monetary landscape.

However, the balance is starting to shift. On a three-month rolling basis, developed markets have moved back toward neutral, suggesting the bar for additional cuts is rising.

The data indicate the previous disinflation phase that made rate cuts straightforward is over, as the energy (USO) (BNO) (UNG) (BOIL) shock from the Middle East conflict drives up producer and consumer prices. Central banks remain net easers, but with far less conviction than before, and some are already being pulled toward more hawkish positioning amid persistent energy concerns.

Emerging markets (EEM) remain further along in the easing cycle than their developed counterparts (VEA), but even there, the trend appears less one-sided than it did just months ago.

data.title: Sempra should spin off its Oncor unit, activist investor Voss says - report
data.description:
Sempra (SRE) should spin off its Texas electric unit, Oncor, activist investor Voss Capital says.

Voss, which owns roughly 2 million shares, or less than 1%, of Sempra (SRE), believes that a newly independent Oncor Electric Delivery Company would be the highest-growth public transmission ​utility in the U.S. and could be worth as much as $78 billion by the end of 2028, according to a  Reuters report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter and a letter seen by the newswire .

Sempra (SRE) controls around 80% of Oncor, and its stake in a spinoff may be worth more than Sempra's current market cap of $59 billion. A spinoff would make sense because Sempra now has three distinct businesses, including its South California utility operations, Oncor and Sempra Infrastructure.

Oncor did not have ‌any immediate ⁠comment for Reuters. Sempra did not respond to a request for comment. Voss declined further comment beyond the letter.

data.title: Wall Street is mixed as eyes remain focused on U.S.-Iran relations
data.description:
Wall Street was mixed on Friday as as traders kept a close watch on any updates related to the U.S.-Iran conflict amid reports of fresh strikes.

The blue chip Dow (DJI) was +0.3%, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) added +0.1%, and the tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) rounded out higher by -0.2%. 

From a sector-by-sector perspective, three of the 11 S&P segments were in the green, led by the info tech space. At the other end of the spectrum, communication services have suffered the most. 

U.S. Treasury yields were muted across the curve. The U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved down 1 basis point to 4.01%. At the same time, the U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved lower by 1 basis point to 4.44%, and the U.S. 30 Year Treasury yield (US30Y) was near even at 4.98%. 

In the geopolitical sphere, the U.S. and Iran have reportedly reached a deal; however, President Donald Trump will have to give the final approval. Reports suggested that Iran had launched missiles. 

On the economic calendar, wholesale inventories' growth slowed less than expected in April, while retail growth came in line. 

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Dell Technologies (DELL) advanced by 28.1%, while shares of Autodesk (ADSK) fell 4.8%.

data.title: UnitedHealth’s unit sued by Massachusetts over alleged $100M Medicaid overpayments
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group’s (UNH) insurance unit, UnitedHealthcare, is facing a lawsuit from Massachusetts Attorney General Andrea Joy Campbell over allegations that the company inflated diagnoses for low-income elderly patients to secure higher government payments.

According to the complaint, UnitedHealthcare received at least $100M more than it should have from the Massachusetts Medicaid program over the past decade by making older adults appear sicker than they actually were, reported Thefly.

The lawsuit claims the company used fraudulent diagnoses to boost reimbursements tied to patients enrolled in both Medicare and Medicaid programs.

The complaint also alleges that pressure to increase revenue and profit in the state’s Medicaid business became so intense that a top Massachusetts executive resigned.

The case is one of the first major lawsuits tied to alleged “upcoding” involving dual-eligible patients, though similar scrutiny has already surrounded Medicare Advantage plans recently.

The allegations could increase regulatory pressure on UnitedHealth Group (UNH) and potentially draw broader attention to Medicaid billing practices across the health insurance industry.

data.title: Quantinuum IPO said to be oversubscribed: report
data.description:
The initial public offering of quantum computing firm Quantinuum (QNT) is oversubscribed by a double-digit multiple of the number of shares available, Bloomberg reported, citing people familiar with the plan.

Quantinuum is owned by industrial conglomerate Honeywell (HON), and its offering is set to occur early next month.

Honeywell and Quantinuum did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Quantinuum recently filed to offer 21.05M shares of its Class A common stock in its initial public offering and sees the price to be between $45 and $50 per share. At the top end of the range, Quantinuum would raise $1.05B and be valued at roughly $13B. The firm plans to apply to list its Class A common stock on the Nasdaq Global Market under the symbol “QNT.”

According to the filing, Quantinuum generated 2025 bookings of $79.3M, with revenue rising to $30.9M from $23.0M in 2024, while net loss widened to $192.6M as the company continued investing heavily in growth and commercialization efforts. Bookings were $1.3M for the three months ended March 31, 2026, compared to $1.9M for the three months ended March 31, 2025.

The firm recently signed a deal with the U.S. federal government to receive R&D funding to address specific technology bottlenecks in the development of fault-tolerant trapped-ion quantum computers.

data.title: Coinbase's futures commission merchant to offer access to global crypto perpetual futures and options
data.description:
Coinbase Global (COIN) said Coinbase Financial Markets has become the first and only U.S.-regulated futures commission merchant that offers access to global crypto derivatives markets, including crypto perpetual futures and options.

A guidance issued by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission positions Coinbase to connect its U.S. clients to global crypto options and perpetual futures liquidity.

"Crypto derivatives account for roughly 80% of global crypto trading volume. Options, perpetual futures, and other instruments drive the majority of that activity across international venues," said Coinbase in a statement published on Friday.

"This is a multi-trillion dollar market in annual trading volume that U.S. customers have had no regulated way to access," added the statement.

data.title: The Las Vegas Strip records its third straight month of gaming win growth
data.description:
Gaming win on the Las Vegas Strip rose 6.6% in April to $689.5M on a year-over-year comparison to mark the third consecutive month of positive growth.

Statewide gaming win was up 5.3% year-over-year in April to $1.30B. Downtown Las Vegas casinos saw gaming win dip 0.6% during the month to $83.4M. Meanwhile, Reno casinos reported gaming win increased 11.8% during the month to $72.3M.

Across the state, gaming win was up 4.1% for the slot machine business on a win percentage of 6.9%. Table/counter/card games gaming win rose 8.3% on a win percentage of 14.2%. Baccarat gaming win was up 20% during the month to help offset a 17.8% decline in roulette gaming win. Sportsbooks saw a 2.6% increase in gaming win to $43.7M.

The biggest April tourism and casino drivers in Las Vegas during the month were WrestleMania 42 at Allegiant Stadium, the NAB Show convention, the NCAA Frozen Four and other sports at T-Mobile, plus a dense slate of residencies and Sphere concerts that filled out weekends and supported gaming volume on the Strip.

On a fiscal year-to-date basis (July 2025-April 2026), Nevada gaming win is up 2.3%, despite an event calendar seen as favorable for conventions and tourism.

Sector watch: Caesars Entertainment (CZR), MGM Resorts (MGM), Boyd Gaming (BYD), Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Full House Resorts (FLL), Bally's Corporation (BALY), VICI Properties (VICI), Sportradar (SRAD), Genius Sports (GENI), Gaming & Leisure Properties (GLPI), and Red Rock Resorts (RRR).

data.title: MongoDB makes strong start to FY27; analysts identify AI upside
data.description:
MongoDB's (MDB) first-quarter results and guidance produced a strong start to fiscal 2027, prompting an array of financial firms to increase their price targets on the stock.

During early post-market action on Thursday, MongoDB's shares shot up more than 20%. However, by early market action on Friday, the cloud database company's shares were down 2%.

"MDB raised its FY27 guidance, which was well above Street estimates across the board as the company looks to capitalize on demand to modernize legacy applications while remaining conservative around Atlas growth into Q4, said Wedbush analysts, led by Dan Ives, in a Friday investor report. "FY27 total revenue is estimated to be in the range of $2.92 billion to $2.96 billion, which was above the Street's $2.90 billion estimate, including Atlas revenue growth of 23% y/y–25% y/y vs. prior guidance of 21% y/y–23% y/y as the company remains conservative given the decreased visibility into future quarters."

Wedbush maintained its Outperform rating and increased its price target to $390 from $380.

"This was a much-needed strong quarter by MDB following its FY4Q26 misstep as the company continues adding more customers that are in the early stages of infrastructure modernization while scaling out AI workloads with MDB representing the essential database for AI," Ives added.

Citi Research retained its Buy rating and inched its hefty price target up to $455 from $450.

"The FY27 revenue guide was raised by +$60M, well in excess of the 1Q outperformance (+25M) and modest M&A impact (~$6M), reflecting strong momentum and visibility in Atlas (200 bps raise) and EA," Citi analysts said in a Friday note. "While the Q2 guide is exceptionally strong (5% above consensus and much stronger QoQ Seasonality), EA is a big driver at +20% Y/Y, though we view the Atlas guide of 26% as impressive still given the 3pt tougher compare and continue to see a pathway to 30%+ growth in Q2. Profitability was also raised, showing incremental discipline and AI efficiencies in the model."

Meanwhile, Mizuho Securities increased its price target to $400 from $325, Piper Sandler raised its target to $400 from $330, Stifel hiked its target up to $435 from $330, and Needham bumped its target 33% higher to $400 from $300.

data.title: AST SpaceMobile is the best performing communication services stock in the past month
data.description:
As the month of May comes to an end, below is a list of the top 10 communication services stocks ranked by their past one-month price performance. The list includes market capitalization data and Quant ratings for each stock within the communication services sector.

The list is topped by AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (ASTS), with an impressive one-month performance of 85.16%. Iridium Communications Inc. (IRDM), Rumble Inc. (RUM), and StubHub Holdings, Inc. (STUB) round out the top four performers, each posting gains above 30%.

While many stocks on the list carry Hold ratings, several have earned more favorable assessments. Sirius XM Holdings Inc. (SIRI) and Reddit, Inc. (RDDT) have both received Buy ratings, with RDDT earning a Quant Rating of 4.37. Warner Music Group Corp. (WMG) stands out with a Strong Buy Quant Rating of 4.52, despite ranking tenth in terms of monthly performance.

Here is the list:

  1. AST SpaceMobile, Inc. (ASTS), 1 month performance percentage: 85.16%

  2. Iridium Communications Inc. (IRDM), 1 month performance percentage: 37.24%

  3. Rumble Inc. (RUM), 1 month performance percentage: 34.95%

  4. StubHub Holdings, Inc. (STUB), 1 month performance percentage: 31.19%

  5. Lumen Technologies, Inc. (LUMN), 1 month performance percentage: 22.71%

  6. Roku, Inc. (ROKU), 1 month performance percentage: 16.75%

  7. Lionsgate Studios Corp. (LION), 1 month performance percentage: 16.69%

  8. Sirius XM Holdings Inc. (SIRI), 1 month performance percentage: 13.75%

  9. Reddit, Inc. (RDDT), 1 month performance percentage: 13.36%

  10. Warner Music Group Corp. (WMG), 1 month performance percentage: 13.31%
